title: westringia glabra
intro: Westringia glabra, also known as the woolly westringia or coastal rosemary, is a flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and grayish-green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective against a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. The medicinal properties of Westringia glabra have been attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, providing various health benefits such as relieving nausea, calming respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. Westringia glabra can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy its numerous health benefits.
